title: ⭐ Practice: Main Idea and Specific Details

questions.prompt:
Read the notice:
"LIBRARY NOTICE
The library will be CLOSED on Monday 15th for staff training. It will reopen on Tuesday 16th at 9 a.m. as usual. We apologize for any inconvenience."

Why is the library closing?
questions.explanation: The notice clearly states the library will be closed "for staff training". Repairs, a public holiday, and bad weather are not mentioned anywhere in the text.

questions.prompt:
Read the email:
"Hi Marco,
I hope you're well. Just a quick note — the team meeting has been moved from Thursday to Friday at 3 p.m. Please let the others know.
Best, Sarah"

What is the main purpose of this email?

options.text: To inform Marco about a change of meeting day
To ask Marco to organise a meeting
To invite Marco to a new project

questions.explanation: Sarah's email informs Marco that the meeting has moved from Thursday to Friday — a change of day, not a cancellation. She doesn't ask Marco to organise anything and there is no mention of a new project.

questions.prompt:
Read the article extract:
"Urban gardens are growing in popularity across Europe. In the last ten years, city councils have created over 500 community growing spaces in major cities. These gardens provide fresh vegetables, improve air quality, and bring neighbours together."

What is the article MAINLY about?

options.text: The rise of urban gardens in European cities
The health benefits of eating vegetables
How to start a community garden
Air pollution problems in European cities

questions.explanation: The main topic is the growing popularity of urban gardens across European cities. Vegetables and air quality are mentioned as benefits but are details, not the main idea. The text does not explain how to start a garden, and pollution is not the focus.

questions.prompt:
Read the article extract:
"Urban gardens are growing in popularity across Europe. In the last ten years, city councils have created over 500 community growing spaces in major cities. These gardens provide fresh vegetables, improve air quality, and bring neighbours together."

How many community growing spaces have been created in the last ten years?
questions.explanation: The text states that city councils have created "over 500 community growing spaces" in the last ten years. 50 and 5,000 are common confusion errors with numbers. The number is clearly given, so option (d) is incorrect.

questions.prompt:
Read the story extract:
"Ali arrived at the station just as the train doors were closing. He ran as fast as he could, but the train pulled away before he could reach it. He stood on the empty platform, breathing hard."

How does Ali probably FEEL at the end?
questions.explanation: Ali missed the train despite running hard — he is likely disappointed (he failed to catch the train) and tired (he ran as fast as he could and is now breathing hard). There is no reason to feel relieved or excited. The text does not mention anger at staff.

questions.prompt:
Read the advert:
"SALSA DANCE CLASSES — Beginners Welcome!
Every Tuesday, 7–9 p.m., Community Centre Room 4.
No partner needed. All ages. First class FREE.
Call 0123 456789 to book your place."

Which statement is TRUE?

options.text: You must bring a dance partner to join.
Classes are held on Wednesday evenings.

questions.explanation: The advert says "First class FREE", so the first lesson costs nothing (c). It also says "No partner needed" (so a is false), classes are on Tuesday (not Wednesday, so b is false), and "All ages" means everyone can attend (d is false).
